title: Stemmacantha centauroides
taxonomy: Stemmacantha centauroides is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is a type of thistle that is native to South Africa. The classification of Stemmacantha centauroides is as follows: kingdom - Plantae, clade - Angiosperms, clade - Eudicots, clade - Asterids, order - Asterales, family - Asteraceae, genus - Stemmacantha, species - S. centauroides. This plant is also known by its synonym, Centaurea centauroides. Further classification information may be found in botanical databases.
uses: Stemmacantha centauroides has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. Its extracts have shown potential in exhibiting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. This plant is also reported to have antimicrobial properties, which may help in treating infections. Traditional medicine practitioners use it to treat various conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues. However, its efficacy and safety for human consumption require further scientific evaluation.

The active constituents of this plant are primarily flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. side_effects: Stemmacantha centauroides, a plant commonly known as the 'cotton thistle', does not have a well-documented history of side effects due to a lack of research on its human consumption. intro: Stemmacantha centauroides, also known as the Centaurea spiny or Devil's thorn, is a perennial plant species in the daisy family Asteraceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Stemmacantha centauroides include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Stemmacantha centauroides is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to promote digestion, reduce pain and inflammation, and support the immune system.
